title: podman exec stdin regression
body:
I discovered a regression bug in podman, since commit a1a79c08b72793cf2f75490d8ffc844c3d16bd4a podman exec does not attach stdin to the command

/kind bug

**Description**

podman doesn't attach stdin to the command when podman exec is used


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
run a container
podman run --rm --detach --name busybox busybox sleep 60

2.
echo test | podman exec -i busybox cat


**Describe the results you received:**

I receive an empty output

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect to receive output "test"


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
it works before commit a1a79c08b72793cf2f75490d8ffc844c3d16bd4a

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.6.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
I think I'm also hitting some stdin issues with `exec`. I thought I remember being able to pipe SQL to a container like I do below. But, now I'm not sure if I'm doing something wrong or if I'm running into this bug.

**Steps to reproduce**
```shell
$ sudo podman run --rm --detach --name mypg docker.io/library/postgres:12.0-alpine
2b8a869654ea7978e39767b903a58574bcd9fec61f644e51bb6b2acb51997baf

$ echo 'select 1;' | sudo podman exec -i mypg psql -U postgres
```

**Expected behavior**
I expected `select 1` to be evaluated by Postgres and the result of that query to be printed to stdout.

**Actual behavior**
```
Error: read unixpacket @->/var/run/libpod/socket/edcc49d484e689c71e4f52206859ddd55a591754356bf05412e7015d1f86450a/attach: 
read: connection reset by peer
```

**Other info**
This does work, however.
```
$ sudo podman exec -i --tty mypg psql -U postgres
psql (12.0)
Type "help" for help.

postgres=# 
```

I have the exact same `podman version` and `podman info --debug` as yanchenko-igor.
I still wouldn't actually consider this closed
Retried with podman 1.6.2, yeah, still broken.
@variadico the partial fixes would be in Master not in a released version.  But @haircommander Says it is not fixed yet anyways.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

Is there any updates to this issue? I'm running into a problem backing up / restoring a mariadb container via:

Backup (Successful): 
`podman exec -it mariadbcontainer mysqldump -u USER -pPASS DATABASE > mybackup.sql`

Restore (Unsuccessful):
`cat mybackup.sql | podman exec -i mariadbcontainer mysql -u USER -pPASS DATABASE`

This is my usual workflow with docker, however, it fails to restore the backup for a podman container.

@rhatdan I view this one as similar in scope to the recent Openstack bug about reusing container names. It's likely a small issue somewhere in the exact details of how Podman and Conmon interact during attach, but identifying the precise where and what exactly is going on are probably going to be very difficult. I'd love to solve this, but I think we'd need a lot of eyes and a fair bit of time blocked off to give it a proper look.
is there any progress with it? without it any database daemon like mysql, mariadb, mongo etc. initialization can't be done when running in a container.

I'm in the process of reworking `podman attach` for the new apiv2 branch, and I'm hoping to get a fix for this in at the same time since I'm knee-deep in the code already, but I don't want to make promises - we still don't have a 100% certain root cause here.

I have also encountered this issue while trying to set up a podman custom executor for GitLab CI following this guide: https://tech.immerda.ch/2019/10/gitlab-ci-with-podman/

This line runs and exits, without taking any action: `podman exec "$CONTAINER_ID" /bin/bash < "$1"`, and adding `-i` results in the same.

I have been able to work around in this way, where `$1` refers to a bash script.
```
CMD=$(cat $1)
podman exec "$CONTAINER_ID" /bin/bash -c "$CMD"
```
Can confirm the fix working as well. Hope this gets into Fedora and RHEL soon.
we're waiting to it also...at least someone can make a scratch-build for fedora and rhel-7 then we'd be happy to test them:-)
